---
title: "Unrefereed Course Notes"
---

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(gt)

note_entries <- c("Title", "Author", "Subject", "Pages", "University", "Contact", "Abstract")

note_data <- c("Number Theory", "Whipple, Kenneth E.", "number theory", "26", "Georgia State University", "", "These notes cover topics usually found in an introductory course in number theory, including divisors, recursion, remainders, greatest common divisors, prime factors, congruence, the Chinese Remainder Theorem, Pythagorean triplets, using residue systems to solve modulo problems, quadratic residues and the Legendre function, reduced residue systems, integral powers, and the totient function.")


tibble(note_entries, note_data) |>
  gt() |>
  cols_align(align = "left") |>
  tab_style(
    style= cell_text(v_align = "top"),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) |>
  tab_options( table.width = "80%", column_labels.hidden = TRUE)  


```
- [Download File](./number-theory_whipple.pdf)

This course guide was donated to a collection originally gathered by MathNerds. The author retains copyright. You are free to use these materials for your classes.
